back: emphatic "yes, I am" (common Brazilian pattern: verb + sim)
short for "não é?" — tag question, like "right?"
"Do you have...?" (using "ter" instead of "haver" — standard in spoken Brazilian PT)
short for "vamos embora" — "let's go!" / "let's do it!"
"get well!" (common farewell when someone is sick)
"I'd like to" (using imperfect for politeness, not "eu quero")
Brazilian instant payment system (everyone uses it)
"you passed it" (dela = de + ela, referring to the street)
"you'll end up right on" (cair = to fall, used figuratively)
Brazilian salsa (tomato, onion, pepper, vinegar) — not the dressing
"how much did it end up being?"
"that's the face of Brazil" / "that's so Brazilian"
main highway in São Paulo (Marginal Pinheiros/Tietê)
"you get used to it" (a gente = informal "we/one")
chocolate lava cake (common Brazilian dessert, French name)
"on the card machine" (delivery person brings it)
Customer Service (Serviço de Atendimento ao Consumidor)
